topics.label: Fortitude
topics.blurb: Courage, resolve and learning — the warrior-scholar's virtues.

topics.label: Justice
topics.blurb: Fairness, mercy and good faith — the just and humane ruler.

topics.label: Severity
topics.blurb: Hardness, force and severity — unbending and formidable.

topics.label: Loyalty
topics.blurb: Loyalty, valour and devotion — the steadfast, generous ally.

topics.label: Vice
topics.blurb: Weakness and corruption — the cautionary, ignoble streak.

people.name: Agrippina the Younger (mother of Nero, wife of Claudius)
Alexander of Heliopolis, governor of Syria under Julian (pagan)
Alexander's mother (ambiguous: could refer to Julia Mamaea, mother of Severus Alexander, or Olympias, mother of Alexander the Great)
Aristides of Athens, Christian apologist (fl. early 2nd century)
Aurelianus, correspondent of Avitus of Vienne and Ennodius of Pavia (early 6th century)
Aureolus, commander of Gallienus' cavalry and usurper (d. 268)
Bassianus, Roman noble and secretary (4th century CE)
Caecilius (old eunuch, trusted messenger in Historia Augusta)
Count Romanus, Roman military official under Valentinian I (4th century)
Diocletian, Roman Emperor (r. 284-305), originally named Diocles
Domitius Modestus, Praetorian Prefect under Valens (fl. 4th century)
Elagabalus (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus), Roman Emperor (r. 218–222)
Eutropius, proconsul of Asia (4th century CE)
Gaius Fabricius Luscinus, Roman consul and envoy to Pyrrhus (early 3rd century BCE)
Gordian I (Marcus Antonius Gordianus Sempronianus Romanus), Roman Emperor (r. 238)
Gordian, Roman Emperor (3rd century, possibly I, II, or III)
Herodes (Fronto's contemporary and Herodes, son of Odaenathus) (pagan)
Hierocles, imperial favorite and official under Elagabalus
Julius Paulus, Roman jurist (early 3rd century CE)
Leontius, governor of Rome and tax assessor at Nicaea (late 4th century)
Longinus (Cassius Longinus), philosopher and rhetorician (c. 213–273 CE)
Lucius Catilius Severus, Roman senator and kinsman of Severus Alexander
Lucius Verus, Roman Emperor (co-emperor with Marcus Aurelius, r. 161-169)
Marcellus, Roman general under Constantius II (fl. mid-4th century)
Marius Victorinus, Roman philosopher and Christian convert (4th century)
Maximinus the Younger (son of Maximinus Thrax, Caesar 236–238 CE, d. 238 CE)
Maximus, philosopher and associate of Emperor Julian
Messalla, consul and associate of Cicero (1st c. BCE)
Mother of Bonosus, usurper against Probus
Proculus, Roman jurist or official (2nd century CE, contemporary of Fronto)
Pyrrhus of Epirus, King of Epirus (r. 306–302, 297–272 BCE)
Sabinus, city prefect under Maximus and Balbinus (Historia Augusta)
Saturninus, Roman military official (fl. 4th century, in Ammianus Marcellinus and Historia Augusta)
Severus, Roman general (4th century, commander at Rheims)
Theodora, Empress of the Eastern Roman Empire (wife of Justinian I, r. 527–548)
Timestheus, Praetorian Prefect and father-in-law of Gordian III

2.name: Agrippina the Younger (mother of Nero, wife of Claudius)

3.name: Diocletian, Roman Emperor (r. 284-305), originally named Diocles

4.name: Elagabalus (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus), Roman Emperor (r. 218–222)
